steps.title: Read the doubling structure
steps.content: The model is $$f(t)=40{,}000\cdot 2^{t/790}.$$ The starting amount is $40{,}000$, and the power of $2$ controls how many doublings happen.

steps.title: Set one doubling
steps.content: For the population to double once, the multiplier must be $2^1$. So set the exponent equal to $1$: $$\frac{t}{790}=1.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Solving gives $t=790$, so choice B is correct. Choice A is the base, choice C is two doubling periods, and choice D is the initial population, not a time.
